text: I want to inline this model (or at least parts of it) in the interst admin view. it has a 1-to-1 with interest. ``` class UserInput(OtovoModel): if TYPE_CHECKING: objects: models.Manager[UserInput] mounting_surface: NullableForeignKey[MountingSurface] interest = models.OneToOneField( to=Interest, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='user_input', unique=True, ) models.ManyToManyField(Photo, through=InterestPhoto) roof_slope = EnumCharField( enum=RoofSlope, default=RoofSlope.NONE, blank=True, max_length=100, ) roof_frame = EnumCharField( enum=RoofFrame, enum=EaveHeight, default=EaveHeight.NONE, blank=True, max_length=100, ) asbestos_in_roof = EnumCharField( enum=AsbestosInRoof, default=AsbestosInRoof.NONE, blank=True, max_length=100, ) consent_to_share_data_with_partner = EnumCharField( enum=ConsentToShareDataWithPartner, default=ConsentToShareDataWithPartner.NONE, blank=True, max_length=100, ) mounting_surface = models.ForeignKey( verbose_name=_('The surface submitted by the customer'), null=True, blank=True, ) yearly_consumption_kwh = models.PositiveIntegerField( blank=True, null=True, ) monthly_energy_bill = MoneyField( blank=True, null=True, max_digits=8, ) # Temporary storage of planning.CustomerInput fields. # We will in the future consolidate these two models into one, but for now # we accept CustomerInput values here and copy them to the project's CustomerInput # when the project is created. customer_input = JSONField(blank=True, null=True, encoder=OtovoJsonEncoder) objects = models.Manager() @staticmethod def custom_form_compatible_fields(): return [ UserInput.asbestos_in_roof.field, UserInput.consent_to_share_data_with_partner.field, UserInput.eave_height.field, UserInput.grid_phase.field, UserInput.installation_placement.field, UserInput.intention_to_buy.field, UserInput.interesting_purchase_models.field, UserInput.monthly_energy_bill.field, UserInput.monthly_energy_consumption.field, UserInput.monthly_energy_spend.field, UserInput.mounting_surface.field, UserInput.photos.field, UserInput.planning_permission.field, UserInput.preferred_subsidy.field, UserInput.property_ownership_status.field, UserInput.roof_slope.field, UserInput.roof_frame.field, UserInput.special_circumstances.field, UserInput.standard_electrical_cabinet.field, UserInput.type_of_area.field, UserInput.yearly_consumption_kwh.field, UserInput.yearly_energy_consumption.field, UserInput.yearly_energy_spend.field, ] def get_all_photos(self) -> QuerySet[InterestPhoto]: """ Returns a QuerySet for getting all photos associated with the project. """ return self.interestphoto_set.all() def update_customer_surface(self, surface: Surface) -> Surface: current_surface = self.surface if current_surface and current_surface == surface: # The current surface is re-submitted. This should not really happen. # Remove the tilings and panels, and start over. Tiling.objects.filter(surface=surface).delete() # The newest surface is set # The old surface is not deleted, but persisted in the 'surface_set' on the address self.surface = surface self.save() return surface def select_correct_panels_from_layout(self, *, tiling: Tiling, panel_ids: list[UUID]) -> None: """ Uses the selected panels list to have the correct tilings. """ # Remove deleted panels Panel.objects.filter(tiling=tiling, tiling__surface=self.surface).exclude(pk__in=panel_ids).update( deleted=True ) # Add all selected panels Panel.objects.filter(tiling=tiling, tiling__surface=self.surface, pk__in=panel_ids).update( deleted=False ) def get_customer_panels(self) -> QuerySet[Panel]: """ Returns the panels from the most recent vertical and horizontal layouts. """ tilings = ( Tiling.objects.filter(surface=self.surface) .distinct('horizontal') .order_by('horizontal', '-created_at') ) return Panel.objects.select_related('tiling__surface').filter(tiling__in=tilings, deleted=False) def get_customer_surface(self) -> QuerySet[Surface]: """ Returns the created customer surface. """ if self.surface: return Surface.objects.filter(pk=self.surface.id) return Surface.objects.none() def get_panel_count(self, tiling: Tiling) -> int: return Panel.objects.filter(tiling__surface=self.surface, tiling=tiling, deleted=False).count() @property def mounting_surface_is_valid(self) -> bool: if not self.mounting_surface: return False return self.mounting_surface.active ``` ```py cloud/cloud/interests/admin.py from django.contrib import admin from django.urls import reverse from django.utils.safestring import mark_safe from cloud.interests.models import Calculation, File, Interest, Offer, PackagePrice, ShoppingCartPackage class CalculationInline(admin.StackedInline): model = Calculation extra = 0 raw_id_fields = ('accepted_price', 'created_by') class OfferInline(admin.StackedInline): model = Offer extra = 0 raw_id_fields = ('contract', 'subsidy', 'created_by', 'package', 'shopping_cart_package') class PackagePriceInline(admin.TabularInline): model = PackagePrice extra = 0 readonly_fields = ('price', 'package') can_delete = False class ShoppingCartPackageInline(admin.TabularInline): model = ShoppingCartPackage extra = 0 readonly_fields = ('shopping_cart', 'package') can_delete = False @admin.register(Calculation) class CalculationAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'created_at' fieldsets = ( ( None, { 'fields': ( 'interest', 'created_by', 'expire_date', 'num_views', 'accepted', 'accepted_price', ) }, ), ( 'Pricing', { 'fields': ( 'cost_model', 'price_model', 'price_error', 'discount_link', ) }, ), ) list_display = ('__str__', 'created_by') list_filter = (('created_by', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter),) list_select_related = ('interest', 'created_by') raw_id_fields = ('accepted_price', 'interest') readonly_fields = ('created_at', 'created_by', 'discount_link') search_fields = ( 'interest__unclean_address', 'interest__email', 'interest__phone_number', ) inlines = (PackagePriceInline, ShoppingCartPackageInline, OfferInline) def discount_link(self, obj): if not obj.discount: return '-' link = reverse( 'admin:discounts_discount_change', args=[obj.discount.id], urlconf='cloud.urls', ) return mark_safe(f'{obj.discount}') def get_queryset(self, request): return ( super() .get_queryset(request) .select_related('interest__address__municipality', 'interest__address__zip_code') ) @admin.register(Offer) class OfferAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'created_at' list_display = ('__str__', 'created_by') list_filter = (('created_by', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter),) list_select_related = ( 'created_by', 'calculation__interest__address__zip_code', 'calculation__interest__address__municipality__county', ) raw_id_fields = ('calculation', 'created_by', 'contract', 'package') readonly_fields = ('created_at',) search_fields = ( 'calculation__interest__unclean_address', 'calculation__interest__email', 'calculation__interest__phone_number', ) class FileInline(admin.StackedInline): model = File extra = 0 raw_id_fields = ('calculation', 'user') @admin.register(File) class FileAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'timestamp' list_display = ('timestamp', 'user', 'file', 'filename') list_filter = ('mime_type', 'aerial') raw_id_fields = ('interest', 'calculation', 'user') search_fields = ( 'interest__unclean_address', 'interest__email', 'interest__phone_number', ) @admin.register(Interest) class InterestAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'created_at' inlines = (CalculationInline, FileInline) list_display = ( 'created_at', 'activated_at', 'won_at', 'address', 'email', 'phone_number', 'customer', 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', 'status', ) list_filter = ( ('lead_qualifier', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter), ('agent', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter), 'status', 'experiment_version', 'calculation_engine', ) list_select_related = ( 'address__municipality__county', 'address__zip_code', 'customer', 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', ) raw_id_fields = ( 'address', 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', 'customer', 'user', 'preferred_surface', 'affiliate_campaign', 'current_shopping_cart', ) readonly_fields = ( 'created_at', 'updated_at', 'activated_at', 'source_type', ) exclude = ('lead',) search_fields = ('unclean_address', 'email', 'phone_number') ``` I'll help you inline the UserInput model in the Interest admin view. Since UserInput has a OneToOne relationship with Interest, you can add it as a StackedInline. Here's how to do it: 1. First, create a UserInputInline class: ```python class UserInputInline(admin.StackedInline): model = UserInput can_delete = False max_num = 1 min_num = 0 extra = 0 fieldsets = ( ('Basic Information', { 'fields': ( 'intention_to_buy', 'interesting_purchase_models', 'property_ownership_status', ) }), ('Energy Information', { 'fields': ( 'monthly_energy_spend', 'monthly_energy_consumption', 'yearly_energy_consumption', 'yearly_energy_spend', 'yearly_consumption_kwh', 'monthly_energy_bill', ) }), ('Installation Details', { 'fields': ( 'installation_placement', 'planning_permission', 'type_of_area', 'roof_slope', 'roof_frame', 'eave_height', 'mounting_surface', 'surface', ) }), ('Technical Details', { 'fields': ( 'grid_phase', 'standard_electrical_cabinet', 'special_circumstances', 'asbestos_in_roof', ) }), ('Other', { 'fields': ( 'preferred_subsidy', 'consent_to_share_data_with_partner', 'customer_input', ) }) ) raw_id_fields = ('mounting_surface', 'surface') ``` 2. Then add it to the InterestAdmin inlines: ```python @admin.register(Interest) class InterestAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'created_at' inlines = (UserInputInline, CalculationInline, FileInline) # Add UserInputInline here # ... rest of your InterestAdmin configuration ... ``` This will: - Show the UserInput form inline within the Interest admin page - Organize the many fields into logical groupings using fieldsets - Use raw_id_fields for the ForeignKey fields - Prevent deletion of the UserInput since it's a required OneToOne relationship - Allow only one UserInput per Interest (max_num=1) You can adjust the fieldsets groupings and their contents to better match your needs. You might also want to add: ```python readonly_fields = ('created_at', 'updated_at') # if these fields exist in your model def has_delete_permission(self, request, obj=None): return False # Prevents deletion of the inline ``` If you want to make certain fields read-only or add custom validation, you can add those to the UserInputInline class as well. If you find the StackedInline takes up too much vertical space, you could alternatively use: ```python class UserInputInline(admin.TabularInline): # ... same configuration as above ... ``` Though given the number of fields, StackedInline is probably more appropriate in this case.
